name: Human idea bank-conflict follow-through: warp-local B consumer transform on the restored accepted base

summary.name: Human idea bank-conflict follow-through: warp-local B consumer transform on the restored accepted base
summary.hypothesis: Round 7 falsified the current L2/block-order clue, and round 6 already falsified the pure fixed-shape Stage rewrite. That leaves the current wall where the earlier diagnoses kept pointing: operand feed on the correct hot-band kernel. The implementation surface has now been restored to the accepted base, so the next move is to keep the same 64x384 outer shape, the same shared footprint, and the same stage depth, but retune only the warp-local B consumer path in the 64x64 PTX micro-tile. The key is to do something stronger than the earlier simple mirrored pair order: for example a lane-aware pair permutation or a shared-address permutation that changes how each warp consumes B fragments without reintroducing CTA repack or a second shared tile.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Warp-local shared/L1 operand delivery and bank behavior on B fragment loads in the true hot-band kernel.
summary.risk: Moderate. This family is correctness-sensitive, and one earlier order-only variant had low signal, so the next attempt has to be more structural than a cosmetic reorder while still preserving the current shared/register budgets.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if the implementation increases shared footprint, adds a CTA-level repack/barrier, or leaves hot-band time flat. This family only stays live while it remains warp-local and shared-neutral.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
